notes: Primary effects in a 1/2 mile (0.8 km) radius; secondary effects (half damage and penalties) in an additional 1D4 miles (1.6–6.4 km). Disaster-specific damage guidelines in Dark Designs pp. 94–95.

prerequisites.summary: Level 7 or greater and a Morphus feature from the Mineral, Plant Life, or Superbeing table.

activationRequirements.summary: 1D4 minute trance communing with nature. If interrupted, P.P.E. is lost and activation fails.

activationTime.summary: 1D4 minute activation trance before the disaster manifests.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Primary 1/2 mile (0.8 km) radius; secondary effects (half damage and penalties) for an additional 1D4 miles (1.6–6.4 km).
rangeAndArea.notes: Disaster type must make sense for the area. No effect on the Astral Plane or Dreamstream.

rules: The Talent user is immune to the destruction — buildings collapse around but not on the user; can walk through burning buildings without harm.
Damage dealt by disasters is natural, not magical, despite magical origin.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: User becomes a priority target for enemies, Nightlords, Avatars, Night Princes/Princesses, Hound Masters, and NSB agents. Using against innocents may drop alignment one step and draw local faction wrath.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Temperature plunges to -6D6°F below freezing within 1D4×10 minutes. 3 in snow or 1 in ice per hour (half in secondary radius). Visibility 5D6 ft; -80% speed, -60% piloting, -80% navigation. Exposure: 6D6+P.E. minutes survival; cumulative -5% skills, -1 attack, -2 combat, -15% speed every 5 minutes (supernatural: 4× norms).

Primary radius rocked; victims crawl at 1/4 speed. 1D4×10+40% non-hardened buildings rubble in 2D4 melees (1D4×10 damage inside; 50% trapped, half for Supernatural P.S.). Secondary: 5D6% collapse (3D6 debris, 30% trapped), half suffer structural damage. No aftershocks.

Wave 4D4+6 ft tall demolishes one-story buildings; 2D6 damage on impact plus 2D6/minute in debris water. Swimming -50%, three successes per melee to reach safety. Drown after 15 sec/P.E. underwater. Residual flood 2D4 ft; secondary 1D4+1 ft minor flooding.

Heavy rain, 200 ft visibility, -30% piloting/navigation. Flyers: -20% speed, 50%/minute lightning strike (6D6, 20% unconscious 1D6 melees). Foot: -20% speed, -1 attack, -1 combat (-2 airborne). 40%/hour chance of 2D4 hail damage/melee for 1D6 melees.

Requires volcano within 50 miles or magma vents in primary radius. Fissures within 10 ft: 2D6 heat/min unprotected; Spd, attacks, combat bonuses, and skills halved from heat/gas. Lava flow: 2D6×10/melee. Eruption: 2D6/melee from ash/rock rain; visibility halved; may start wildfires.

Engulfs primary radius in 6D6 minutes. Unshielded: 5D6 flame + 3D6 smoke/melee; -80% visibility. 80% escape chance if flee immediately (-10%/melee hesitation). Urban shelter-in-place 30% survival (10% elsewhere). 6D6% buildings gutted/hour unchecked (halve with swift response). Secondary: 4D6 total damage/melee in smaller fires.

environmentalConstructs.summary: Localized natural disaster — blizzard, earthquake/landslide, flash flood/tsunami, thunderstorm, volcanic eruption, or wildfire. Alternate stats in Dead Reign: Hell Followed.

combatMechanics.notes: Secondary radius suffers half damage and penalties. Duration 15 minutes per user level or until slain or canceled.

statusAlterations.summary: Wracks the landscape with a chosen natural disaster while the user remains untouched.

name: Necrosis
description: Inflicts grievous damage on living flesh by accelerating natural decay. The target must have unhealed wounds or a stigmata — recent injuries or wounds still healing from days or weeks prior; not old scars or missing limbs. Failed saves necrotize wounds and surrounding tissue. Biomechanical, Plant, and Alien morphuses suffer half damage and penalties; Mineral morphuses are immune. Nightbane and other regenerators temporarily lose healing for five minutes per level of the Talent user (psionic/magical healing still works).

enhancement.summary: Every two P.P.E. points spent inflicts 1D6 damage (S.D.C. then Hit Points).

ppe.notes: Every two P.P.E. spent inflicts 1D6 damage up to maximum 1D6×10 (10D6). P.P.E. amount selected BEFORE the victim saves; full cost spent whether save succeeds or fails.

limitations.otherLimitations: Living creatures only. Wampyrs and Nightbane with Artificial Appearance, Biomechanical, or Substance Abuse features are +2 to save. Maximum damage 1D6×10. Each wound may only be affected once, but different wounds on the same target can be attacked separately. A pre-existing wound, cut, scratch, bullet wound, or bite must exist before use.

ranges.summary: Touch or up to 10 feet (3.1 m) per level of experience; line of sight required.

duration.summary: Instant effect and damage with lingering penalties.

save.summary: Save vs magic 15 or greater.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: 1D6 damage per P.P.E. point spent (S.D.C. then Hit Points). Feverish: -2 attacks per melee; combat bonuses, Spd, and skills halved for 1D6 melee rounds; -3 to save vs disease for 1D4 hours. Regeneration shut off for 5 minutes per Talent-user level.
save.effectsOnSuccessfulSave: No damage or attack loss; heal/regenerate normally, but -1 all combat rolls and -5% skills for 1D6 melee rounds.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one feature from the Undead, Nightmare, Pestilence, or Stigmata table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch or up to 10 feet (3.1 m) per level; line of sight required.

activationTime.summary: Instant — P.P.E. amount (2 per 1D6 damage, max 1D6×10) selected before the victim saves.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: 1D6 damage to S.D.C. then Hit Points per 2 P.P.E. spent (maximum 1D6×10). Full P.P.E. spent whether the save succeeds.
attacksAndDamage.notes: Requires a pre-existing unhealed wound, cut, scratch, bullet wound, bite, or stigmata. Each wound may only be affected once.

offensiveDamage.notes: Damage applies to S.D.C. first, then Hit Points.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Wampyrs and Nightbane with Artificial Appearance, Biomechanical, or Substance Abuse features save at +2.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Wounds necrotize; damage per P.P.E. spent; feverish penalties; regeneration shut off for 5 minutes per user level (psionic/magical healing still works).
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: No damage or attack loss; -1 all combat rolls and -5% skills for 1D6 melee rounds.

statusEffects.summary: Biomechanical, Plant, and Alien morphuses suffer half damage and penalties. Mineral morphuses are immune.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: On failed save: combat bonuses, Spd, and skills halved for 1D6 melee rounds; -3 save vs disease for 1D4 hours. On successful save: -1 strike/parry/dodge and -5% skills for 1D6 melee rounds.

statusEffects.summary: Regenerators lose natural healing/regeneration for 5 minutes per Talent-user level.

statusAlterations.summary: Accelerates decay on existing wounds — surrounding tissue necrotizes.

name: Nightshade
description: Upon activation, releases a chemical scent into the air that debilitates everyone who inhales it. Failed save vs non-lethal poison (16+): deep comatose sleep for up to 2D4+2 minutes. Successful saves still suffer -1 attack per melee, -2 on all combat rolls, and -10% to all skills. Anyone under the sleep effect who is attacked immediately awakens and may defend with full bonuses.

ppe.notes: Eleven P.P.E. to activate with immediate effect.

limitations.otherLimitations: If someone under the effects of this Talent is attacked, they immediately awaken and can defend with full bonuses.

ranges.summary: 20 foot (6 m) radius around the Nightbane, +5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience

duration.summary: Failed save: comatose sleep up to 2D4+2 minutes. Successful save: penalties last for the exposure.

save.summary: Save vs non-lethal poison 16 or higher. Failed save: deep sleep. Successful save: -1 attack per melee, -2 on all combat rolls, -10% to all skills.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Plant Life or Extraterrestrial characteristic.

rangeMechanics.summary: 20 foot (6 m) radius around the Nightbane, +5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

rangeAndArea.areaOfEffectSummary: 20 foot (6 m) radius chemical scent, +5 feet (1.5 m) per user level.

activationTime.summary: 11 P.P.E. to activate with immediate effect.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: 16 or higher to save. Failed save: comatose sleep up to 2D4+2 minutes. Successful save: partial penalties still apply.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Partial save penalties last for the exposure. Failed save: deep sleep until duration ends or victim is attacked.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Deep comatose sleep for up to 2D4+2 minutes.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: -1 attack per melee, -2 on all combat rolls, -10% to all skills.
savingThrow.notes: Everyone who inhales the scent must save. Attacking a sleeping victim immediately awakens them with full defensive bonuses.

statusEffects.name: Nightshade Sleep
statusEffects.summary: Comatose deep sleep for up to 2D4+2 minutes on failed save.

statusAlterations.summary: Chemical scent released into the air — debilitating to all who inhale it within the radius.

name: No Rest
description: Like All-Nighter, enables long stretches without sleep and without chemical stimulants. On self, eliminates sleep-deprivation effects for one day per level, keeps the user fully awake and alert, grants +1 Perception, and +5 to save vs sedatives and sleep-inducing powers. As an attack (unlike All-Nighter), may be used on unwilling targets who fail save vs magic 14+ — they fatigue normally but cannot sleep. Cumulative penalties per 24 hours without sleep; after 72 hours regeneration halves; after 120 hours regeneration stops; after 168 hours daily insanity saves; 13 days triggers Random Insanity Table. Effects can be canceled by the user at any distance; sleep magic/psionics force a save to counteract. Usable in a Dream Pool to awaken the dreamer and expel intruders without a save.

enhancement.summary: +3 extra P.P.E. to raise the saving throw by one point (max 15).
enhancement.stepDefinition: +1 to the target's saving throw difficulty

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per use. +3 extra P.P.E. to raise the saving throw by one point (maximum 15).

ongoingUse.notes: Effects continue even when the user shifts to Facade.

limitations.otherLimitations: Self-use removes physical need for rest for days equal to M.E.; beyond that, daily save vs insanity (temporary insanities clear after 8+ hours sleep). Long-term self-use or offensive use on others takes a psychological toll.

ranges.summary: Self and one other by touch with good intentions. Touch or 30 feet (9.1 m) when used on one victim as an attack.

duration.summary: 24 hours per level of experience.

save.summary: Unwilling targets save vs magic 14 or better (+3 P.P.E. per +1 to save difficulty, maximum 15).
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Target cannot sleep. Per 24 hours without sleep: -1 melee attack, -1 Perception/initiative/all combat/saves, -10% all skills (cumulative). After 72 hours regeneration halved; after 120 hours regeneration stops; after 168 hours daily insanity save 16+; 13 days roll on Random Insanity Table. Penalties halved and insanities clear after 10+ hours sleep; full restore after two consecutive 8-hour sleep periods (50% chance one insanity remains if deprivation lasted 10+ days).

notes: While active on self: +5 to save vs sedatives and sleep-inducing powers. Effects can be canceled by the user at any distance. Usable in a Dream Pool to awaken the dreamer and expel intruders without a save.

prerequisites.summary: Level 3 or greater and at least one trait from the Occupation, Soldier, Ancient Warrior, or Superbeing table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self or one other by touch (willing). Touch or 30 feet (9.1 m) when used as an attack on one victim.

activationTime.summary: 10 P.P.E. per use; +3 P.P.E. per +1 to the target's saving throw (maximum 15).

powerModes.effectsDescription: Eliminates sleep-deprivation effects for one day per level; user remains fully awake and alert. +1 Perception and +5 to save vs sedatives and sleep-inducing powers. Physical need for rest removed for days equal to M.E.; beyond that, daily save vs insanity (temporary insanities clear after 8+ hours sleep).

Unwilling target who fails save vs magic 14+ (max 15) fatigues normally but cannot sleep. Cumulative deprivation penalties per 24 hours; regeneration halved at 72 hours, stops at 120 hours; daily insanity saves after 168 hours; Random Insanity Table at 13 days. Sleep magic/psionics force an automatic counter save.
Used in a Dream Pool to immediately awaken the dreamer and expel intruders — no saving throw.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Self/willing mode only: +1 Perception while active.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Self/willing mode: eliminates sleep-deprivation effects and keeps the user fully awake and alert for the duration.

statusAlterations.summary: Self/willing: staves off sleep deprivation; user remains fully awake and alert without chemical stimulants.

statusEffects.name: Cannot Sleep
statusEffects.summary: Attack mode: target fatigues normally but is completely unable to sleep until the effect is canceled or countered.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Attack mode only. After 72 hours all regeneration halved; after 120 hours regeneration stops; after 168 hours daily save vs insanity 16+ (failed save: roll Phobia or Obsession each subsequent day). 13 days without sleep: roll on Random Insanity Table (Nightbane RPG). Penalties halved and insanities clear after 10+ hours sleep; fully restored after two consecutive nights/days of at least 8 hours sleep (50% chance one insanity remains if deprivation lasted 10+ days).

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Self-use beyond M.E. days requires daily save vs insanity (temporary insanities clear after 8+ hours sleep). Long-term self-use or offensive use on others takes a psychological toll.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Unwilling targets only. +3 P.P.E. per +1 to save difficulty (maximum 15). Sleep magic/psionics used to counteract force an automatic appropriate save.
Attack-mode victims after 168 hours without sleep: daily save; failed save rolls Phobia or Obsession each subsequent day. 13 days: Random Insanity Table.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Target cannot sleep; cumulative deprivation penalties apply per 24 hours (see penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters).
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: No effect.
savingThrow.notes: User may cancel effects at any distance. Dream Pool awakening mode requires no save.

name: Overdrive
description: Drastically increases maximum speed of the user or conveyance to 500 mph (800 kph). The Morphus reconfigures — aerodynamic shape, thrusters, rocket roar or supercharger whine. +2 Perception when looking ahead for trouble and +2 dodge while in motion. Vehicle/animal pilots gain +30% to appropriate Piloting and halve high-speed maneuver penalties. Objects under 10 lbs (4.5 kg) are harmlessly knocked aside; collisions over that inflict 1D4×10 to user/vehicle and target and cancel Overdrive. No physical strain or surface damage at speed unless crash; any flat surface including water works like dry pavement. Supports flight, swimming, running, or driving. On lapse, speed returns to normal within a half mile (0.8 km) in 2–3 seconds.

enhancement.summary: +8 P.P.E. per additional 100 miles (160 km) traveled.

ppe.notes: Sixteen P.P.E. per 100 miles (160 km) traveled. Double P.P.E. for vehicles with up to 4 passengers or 800 lbs (360 kg) cargo; triple for vehicles carrying 20 passengers or 10 tons of cargo.

limitations.otherLimitations: Combat largely limited to dodging, firing ahead, or ramming. Vehicle altitude/depth limits, S.D.C., and engine requirements unchanged. All-or-nothing speed: 500 mph (800 kph) or normal speed — nothing in between without canceling.

ranges.summary: Self, riding animal or vehicle by touch.

duration.summary: Special — lasts for distance traveled; see P.P.E. costs per 100 miles.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +30% to appropriate Piloting skill; halve penalties for high-speed maneuvers when piloting a vehicle or riding animal.

prerequisites.summary: Level 4 or greater and at least one Morphus feature from the Biomechanical, Aquatic Biomech, Superbeing, or Sci-Fi table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self, riding animal, or vehicle by touch.

activationTime.summary: 16 P.P.E. per 100 miles (160 km) traveled; +8 P.P.E. per additional 100 miles.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Maximum speed boosted to 500 mph (800 kph) — all or nothing; no intermediate speed without canceling. Supports flight, swimming (on or underwater), running, or driving. Any flat surface including water is traversed as dry pavement. No physical strain or surface damage at speed unless crash.
mobilityBlocks.notes: On lapse or cancel, speed returns to normal within a half mile (0.8 km) in 2–3 seconds. Objects under 10 lbs (4.5 kg) are harmlessly knocked aside.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: 1D4×10 to the Nightbane or Overdrive vehicle/animal and whatever they collide with.
attacksAndDamage.notes: Collision with anything over 10 lbs (4.5 kg) immediately cancels Overdrive. Combat is largely limited to dodging, firing ahead, or ramming.

damage.summary: Collision with objects over 10 lbs (4.5 kg): 1D4×10 to user/vehicle and target; Overdrive canceled immediately.

combatMechanics.notes: While active, combat is largely limited to dodging incoming attacks, firing at something ahead, or ramming (collision damage above). Vehicle altitude/depth limits, S.D.C., and engine requirements unchanged.

passengerLimits.notes: Double P.P.E. cost for vehicles with up to 4 passengers or 800 lbs (360 kg) cargo; triple for vehicles carrying 20 passengers or 10 tons of cargo.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: All-or-nothing speed: 500 mph (800 kph) or normal speed — nothing in between without canceling. Vehicle must still have a working engine/drivetrain; altitude/depth restrictions unchanged.

statusAlterations.summary: Morphus reconfigures for high-speed travel — aerodynamic shape, thrusters, rocket roar or supercharger whine. Concealed thruster ports may open and fire.

grantedSkills.notes: When piloting a vehicle or riding animal under Overdrive; high-speed maneuver penalties are halved.

name: Pariah's Mantle
description: Draws the ire of others to the user — useful for distraction or protecting weaker allies. Selected targets in range must save 15 or better (including M.E. bonuses) or be overcome with revulsion and violent hatred. They threaten, insult, and assault within 1D4 melee rounds (immediately if they already disliked the user). Bystanders focus on the pariah and assailants; stealthy allies gain cover — relevant Perception Rolls are -6.

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per minute of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Anyone affected who is harmed in any way is immediately released and immune for 24 hours. Good alignment targets get +2 to save; total pacifists get +5.

ranges.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) +5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute per P.P.E. spent.

save.summary: Save 15 or better (M.E. bonus only; good alignment +2, pacifists +5).
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Revulsion and violent hatred; threats, insults, and physical assault within 1D4 melee rounds (immediate if already hostile).

notes: Bystanders and observers focus on the pariah; allies on stealth/Prowl tasks enjoy -6 to enemy Perception Rolls that might notice them.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Morphus feature from the Disproportion, Unusual Facial Feature, or Clown table.

rangeMechanics.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience; Nightbane selects which targets within range are affected.

rangeAndArea.summary: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level; user selects intended targets within range.

activationTime.summary: 1 P.P.E. per minute of use.

alignmentGatedEffects.summary: Good alignment targets get +2 to save.

statusEffects.name: Revulsion and Violent Hatred
statusEffects.summary: Failed save: overcome with revulsion and violent hatred toward the Talent user; threaten, insult, and assault within 1D4 melee rounds (immediate if already hostile).

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Affected targets focus aggression on the pariah. Anyone harmed in any way is immediately released and immune for 24 hours.

bonusesOnAlliedTargets.notes: Allies on Prowl or stealth tasks: enemy Perception Rolls that might notice them are -6 while bystanders focus on the pariah and assailants.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: Anyone affected who already disliked or wanted to attack the character assaults immediately instead of waiting 1D4 melee rounds.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: M.E. exceptional bonus only. Good alignment +2; total pacifists +5.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Revulsion and violent hatred; threats, insults, and physical assault within 1D4 melee rounds (immediate if already hostile).
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: No effect.
savingThrow.notes: M.E. exceptional bonus only. Anyone harmed while affected is immediately released and immune for 24 hours.

combatMechanics.notes: Bystanders and observers focus on the pariah; stealthy allies benefit from -6 enemy Perception Rolls.

statusAlterations.summary: Draws the ire of selected targets and bystanders toward the Talent user — useful for distraction or protecting weaker allies.

name: Passing the Torch
description: Turn ordinary mortals into Nightbane temporarily — or, in rare approved cases, permanently. Temporary use: a willing recipient undergoes the Becoming in an exact copy of the Talent user's Morphus (or GM may assign a new Morphus). They count as Nightbane for targeted effects, with Mirror Walk, sense fellow Nightbane, and Talents copied from the user or selected as level 1. Shock penalties apply even when forewarned. While active, the Talent user is limited to Facade form and reverts immediately on activation. Ends if the recipient is slain (Morphus returns; user knows) or when canceled. Permanent Passing the Torch requires willing certainty, GM approval, at least 20 P.P.E. available, and kills the Talent user — the recipient becomes a full Nightbane retaining Facade attributes, skills, and level.

activationTiers.summary: Permanent torch passing (requires GM approval; kills the Talent user)

ppe.notes: Twelve P.P.E. per hour for temporary Morphus lending. Permanent Passing the Torch requires at least 20 P.P.E. available, GM approval, and kills the Talent user; the new Nightbane permanently spends 12 P.P.E. from the inherited pool.

ongoingUse.notes: While temporary Passing the Torch is active, the Talent user is limited to Facade form and reverts immediately on activation.

limitations.otherLimitations: Willing targets only — unwilling or incapacitated targets automatically save. Only mortal races without psychic abilities.

ranges.summary: Touch or 10 feet (3 m).

duration.summary: Temporary: paid per hour while Morphus is lent. Permanent transformation is irrevocable.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Temporary Nightbane: exhilarating and disorienting Becoming shock even when the recipient knows what to expect.

notes: Permanent torch-passing is an exceedingly rare deathbed sacrifice. New permanent Nightbane inherits Morphus abilities and P.P.E. (minus 12 permanently expended) but keeps own Facade stats and knowledge.

prerequisites.summary: Level 7 or greater and one or more features from the Superbeing, Athlete, or Stigmata tables.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch or 10 feet (3 m).

activationTime.summary: 12 P.P.E. per hour for temporary Morphus lending.

activationRequirements.summary: Willing mortal targets only — no psychic abilities. Unwilling or incapacitated targets automatically save.

ppe.notes: Twelve P.P.E. per hour while the recipient operates in the Talent user's Morphus.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Willing recipient immediately undergoes the Becoming in an exact copy of the user's Morphus (or GM may assign a new Morphus). Recipient counts as a Nightbane with Mirror Walk, sense fellow Nightbane, and Talents copied from the user or selected as level 1. Becoming shock: -1 melee attack, -1 all combat rolls, -10% skill performance. Ends if recipient is slain (Morphus returns; user knows) or when canceled.

ppe.notes: Requires at least 20 P.P.E. available, willing certainty, and GM approval. Kills the Talent user.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Deathbed sacrifice — irrevocable. Recipient becomes a full Nightbane retaining Facade attributes, skills, and level. Inherits Morphus abilities and P.P.E. minus 12 permanently expended. New Nightbane resembles the donor who passed appearance/Morphus and powers on.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Temporary recipient is a Nightbane for all intents: Mirror Walk, sense fellow Nightbane, affected by Nightbane-specific spells and effects. Talents may be copied from the donor or selected as a level 1 Nightbane (player/GM choice).

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: While temporary Passing the Torch is active, the Talent user is limited to Facade form and reverts immediately when the Talent is activated.

attributeCost.summary: Permanent mode: kills the Talent user. Recipient permanently spends 12 P.P.E. from the inherited pool.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: N/A — unwilling or incapacitated targets automatically save.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Willing mortal without psychic abilities may receive the Morphus.
savingThrow.notes: Only willing targets; cannot affect psychics, supernatural beings, or creatures of magic.

combatMechanics.notes: If the temporary recipient is slain, the Morphus returns to the Talent user who knows the recipient died. User may cancel at any time.

statusAlterations.summary: Temporary: recipient manifests an exact copy of the donor's Morphus (or a GM-assigned new Morphus). Permanent: recipient becomes a full Nightbane bearing the donor's Morphus legacy.

statusEffects.name: Becoming Shock
statusEffects.summary: Temporary Nightbane: -1 melee attack, -1 on all combat rolls, -10% on skill performance even when forewarned.

name: Payday
description: Similar to Pocket Change, conjures money from thin air. Ten physical dollars (or local equivalent) appear per 1 P.P.E. spent — real currency that passes forgery inspection and works at registers and vending machines. The Nightbane chooses coin or note denominations up to the P.P.E. spent, in the currency of the country they are in. Created currency lasts longer than Pocket Change but vanishes from pockets, tills, and vaults when the duration expires. Alternatively, the user may conjure 100 dollars per P.E. point expended (24 hours to recover each P.E. point; adjust bonuses and saves accordingly).

activationTiers.summary: P.P.E. conjuration: 10 dollars (or local equivalent) per 1 P.P.E.
P.E. conjuration: 100 dollars per P.E. point expended
activationTiers.stepDefinition: per P.E. point; 24 hours to recover each P.E. point

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per 10 dollars (or local equivalent) created. Alternatively, conjure 100 dollars per P.E. point expended (same duration; 24 hours to recover each P.E. point; adjust bonuses and saves accordingly).

limitations.otherLimitations: Must picture the currency to create it — familiarity with that money type is required (e.g. a Nightbane in Europe needs to know the Euro and/or have an example in hand).

ranges.summary: Self

duration.summary: One hour per level of experience.

createdObjects.name: Physical Currency
createdObjects.summary: Ten dollars (or local equivalent) per 1 P.P.E., or 100 dollars per P.E. point expended. Real currency that passes forgery inspection; works at registers and vending machines. Vanishes from pockets, tills, and vaults when duration expires.

prerequisites.summary: Level 3 or greater and at least one feature from the Occupation, Athlete, or Hobbyist table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self — currency appears in the user's hand.

activationTime.summary: Instant upon P.P.E. or P.E. expenditure.

activationRequirements.summary: Must picture the currency to create it — familiarity with that money type is required (e.g. know the Euro and/or have an example in hand).

ppe.notes: 1 P.P.E. per 10 dollars (or local equivalent). User chooses coin or note denominations up to P.P.E. spent, in local currency.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Ten physical dollars appear in hand per 1 P.P.E. spent. Currency is real, passes forgery inspection, and works at cash registers and vending machines.
Conjure 100 dollars per P.E. point expended. Same money duration; takes 24 hours to recover each P.E. point spent (adjust bonuses and saves accordingly).

attributeCost.summary: P.E. conjuration mode: 100 dollars per P.E. point expended; 24 hours to recover each P.E. point.

statusAlterations.summary: Conjures physical currency from thin air. Created money lasts longer than Pocket Change but vanishes from pockets, tills, and bank vaults when Payday expires.

name: Rallying Cry
description: A supernatural battlefield or locker-room rallying cry that lifts allies and supporters. The cry may be historical, athletic, or pop-cultural — effects are the same. Allies in range gain +2 initiative, +1 strike/parry/dodge, +1 to all saving throws, and +5% to all skills. Allies under demoralizing or manipulating effects (failed Horror Factor, psionic mind control, etc.) immediately reroll the save with the new bonus. Optionally, expend double P.P.E. on specific allies so they reroll against negative effects or penalty saves (14+ including applicable bonuses for non-save penalties such as injury modifiers).

activationTiers.summary: Penalty-removal Rallying Cry on specific allies (double P.P.E. per targeted ally)

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per Rallying Cry. Optionally expend 12 P.P.E. (double) on specific allies for penalty-removal rerolls.

limitations.otherLimitations: User must have a general idea of each target's presence and location. Bonuses do NOT accumulate with multiple uses.

ranges.summary: Every ally or supporter within 10 feet (3.1 m) radius per level of experience, or specific people within range and line of sight.

areaOfEffect.summary: 10 foot (3.1 m) radius per level of experience.

duration.summary: Cry is instantaneous; bonuses last one melee round per level of the Talent user's experience.

notes: Allies affected: +2 initiative, +1 strike/parry/dodge, +1 all saves, +5% skills. Double-P.P.E. option grants reroll vs existing penalties or save 14+ vs non-save penalties for the duration.

bonusesOnAlliedTargets.notes: Bonuses do not accumulate with multiple uses. Allies under demoralizing or manipulating effects immediately reroll the save with these bonuses.

retroactiveSaveGrants.summary: Allies under demoralizing or manipulating effects (failed Horror Factor, psionic mind control, etc.) immediately reroll with Rallying Cry bonuses.
retroactiveSaveGrants.versusPreExistingEffectSummary: Optional double P.P.E. (12) on specific allies: reroll vs negative effects; penalties without a save require 14+ including applicable bonuses (e.g. P.E. for injury penalties).

prerequisites.summary: At least one feature from the Ancient Warrior, Athlete, or Soldier table.

rangeMechanics.summary: 10 foot (3.1 m) radius per level of experience, or specific allies within range and line of sight.

rangeAndArea.summary: Every ally or supporter within 10 feet (3.1 m) radius per level, or specific people within range and line of sight.

activationTime.summary: Instantaneous cry; bonuses last one melee round per user level.

activationRequirements.summary: User must have a general idea of each target's presence and location.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Allies in area gain +2 initiative, +1 strike/parry/dodge, +1 to all saving throws, and +5% to all skills. Demoralized or mind-controlled allies immediately reroll affected saves with the new bonus.

ppe.notes: Double P.P.E. per specifically targeted ally.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Targeted allies reroll against negative effects from enemy powers, injuries, poison side effects, etc. Success ignores penalties for the Talent's duration. Penalties without a saving throw require 14+ including applicable bonuses (e.g. P.E. for Optional Damage Chart injury penalties).

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Applies to affected allies for one melee round per level of the Talent user. +1 to all saving throws and +5% to all skills.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Double-P.P.E. penalty-removal mode: penalties without a formal save (e.g. Optional Damage Chart injury modifiers) require 14+ including applicable bonuses such as P.E.

savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Targeted ally ignores imposed penalties for the duration of Rallying Cry.
savingThrow.notes: Standard mode: demoralized/manipulated allies immediately reroll with +1 save bonus. Penalty-removal mode: reroll vs existing negative effects; non-save penalties use 14+ with applicable bonuses.

combatMechanics.notes: Bonuses granted do not stack with multiple Rallying Cry uses.

statusAlterations.summary: Brief surge of energy and renewed confidence — cry may be historical, athletic, or pop-cultural; effects are identical regardless of wording.

name: Revolting Spit
description: Inspired by caustic dinosaur venom, the Nightbane secretes mild organic acid held in the mouth for a bite or spit. Each use counts as two melee attacks (conjure, then bite or spit). Bite deals normal Morphus damage plus acid. Spit: called shot to the face requires natural roll 14+ (no strike bonuses); may be parried with a shield or hand/forearm. Acid does 1D4 damage initially plus +1 per round for 1D4 melee rounds. Face hit with failed save vs non-lethal poison 13+: blindness and severe fumes penalties. Water neutralizes acid immediately; washing takes 1D4 melee rounds.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per use (conjure acid + bite or spit); counts as two melee attacks.

cannotAffect: inorganic materials (glass, ceramics, crystal, stone, plastic, metal)
Nightbane in full environmental suit or armor
limitations.otherLimitations: Gas mask covering eyes, nose, and mouth prevents penalties. Goggles/visors prevent blindness until removed; fumes still apply. Water neutralizes acid. Clothing and natural fabrics take damage; plants and wood take damage.

ranges.summary: Bite or 10 feet (3.1 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Instant attack; damage and penalties last 1D4 melee rounds or until washed away.

damage: 1D4 initially, +1 per melee round for next 1D4 rounds; double to Nightlords and Nightlands denizens; double (half to Hit Points) on exposed cuts of 3+ damage or vulnerable tissue

save.summary: Save vs non-lethal poison 13 or higher on face hit; called shot natural 14+ required to hit the face.
save.effectsOnSuccessfulSave: -1 melee attack, -1 all combat rolls, -2 Perception, -10% skills (eyes water; fumes hinder breathing).
save.effectsOnFailedSave: 1D4 damage; temporarily blinded (-10 all combat rolls, -80% sight-based skills) plus fumes: -2 melee attacks, -2 combat, -2 Perception, -20% skills for 1D4 melee rounds.

notes: Acid may be held in the mouth until used (prevents speech; may drool). Useful for blinding surveillance cameras until washed off.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: 1D4 damage initially, plus +1 point of additional damage per each of the next 1D4 melee rounds. Double damage to Nightlords, their minions, and Denizens of the Nightlands. Double damage (half direct to HP) on exposed cuts or vulnerable tissue.
attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Counts as two melee attacks (one to conjure, one to bite or spit).

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Face hit only. Called Shot requires natural roll 14+ (no strike bonuses). Victim may parry with shield, shield-like object, hand, or forearm.

statusAlterations.summary: If hit in the face and victim saves: Eyes water and fumes make breathing difficult. -1 melee attack, -1 to all combat rolls, -2 on Perception Rolls, and -10% to all skills.

If hit in the face and victim fails to save: Temporarily blinded and overwhelmed by noxious fumes. -10 to all combat rolls, -80% to sight-based skills (0% if sight is required). Additionally, fumes cause -2 melee attacks, -2 to all combat rolls, -2 on Perception Rolls, and -20% to skill performance for 1D4 melee rounds.

prerequisites.summary: Level 2 or greater and one or more features from the Dinosaur, Snake, Extraterrestrial, or Mythical Creature tables.

rangeMechanics.summary: Bite (melee) or spit up to 10 feet (3.1 m) per level of experience.

activationTime.summary: Two melee attacks per use — first to conjure acid, second to bite or spit.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Normal Morphus bite damage plus acid damage (1D4 initially, +1 per melee round for 1D4 rounds). Acid may be held in the mouth until used.
Ranged acid spit. Called Shot to the face requires natural roll 14+ (no strike bonuses). May be parried with shield, shield-like object, hand, or forearm.

bonusDamageVsNightlandsDenizens.summary: Double damage to Nightlords and Denizens of the Nightlands.

damageModifiersNote: Double damage (half direct to Hit Points) on exposed cuts of 3+ damage or other vulnerable tissue (e.g. inside of throat). G.M. may use Optional Side-Effects Table (Nightbane RPG, p. 39) with player benefit of the doubt.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.attacksPerMelee: -1 on successful save vs face hit; -2 on failed save (plus blindness penalties)
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.strike: -1 on successful save; -10 on failed save (blindness)
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.parry: -1 on successful save; -10 on failed save (blindness)
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.dodge: -1 on successful save; -10 on failed save (blindness)
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.perception: -2 on successful save; -2 additional on failed save from fumes
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.percentToAllSkills: -10 on successful save; -80% sight-based skills on failed save (0% if sight required)
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Failed save face hit: fumes penalties -2 melee attacks, -2 combat, -2 Perception, -20% skills for 1D4 melee rounds. Washing acid away and flushing eyes takes 1D4 melee rounds; water immediately neutralizes acid.

statusEffects.name: Temporary Blindness
statusEffects.summary: Failed save on face hit: -10 to all combat rolls; sight-based skills -80% (0% if sight required). Goggles/visors prevent blindness until removed; fumes still apply.
statusEffects.durationSummary: 1D4 melee rounds or until washed away

statusEffects.name: Noxious Fumes
statusEffects.summary: Failed save: -2 melee attacks, -2 combat, -2 Perception, -20% skills. Successful save: -1 attack, -1 combat, -2 Perception, -10% skills.
statusEffects.durationSummary: 1D4 melee rounds or until washed away

holdsAndEscapes.summary: Spit may be parried with a shield of any size, shield-like object, hand, or forearm.
holdsAndEscapes.notes: Called Shot to the face: natural 14+ required; normal strike bonuses do not apply.

rules: Water completely neutralizes the acid, immediately stopping damage and penalties.
Gas mask covering eyes, nose, and mouth prevents penalties. Goggles/visors prevent blindness until removed.

damage.summary: 1D4 initial acid damage, +1 per melee round for 1D4 rounds. Double to Nightlords and Nightlands denizens. Double (half to Hit Points) on exposed cuts of 3+ damage or vulnerable tissue.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: 1D4 damage; temporary blindness and severe fume penalties for 1D4 melee rounds.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: -1 melee attack, -1 all combat rolls, -2 Perception, -10% skills.
savingThrow.notes: Face hit requires Called Shot natural 14+. Spit may be parried. Surveillance camera lenses remain blind until spit is washed off.

combatMechanics.notes: Acid held in mouth prevents speech and may cause drool until used.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: While acid is held in the mouth: cannot speak; may drool.

name: Rigor Mortis
description: Mimics rigor mortis — stiffening after death. On self or willing targets (Facade or Morphus): appears completely dead with no detectable heartbeat, breathing, or brain activity on modern medical equipment; suspended animation (5% oxygen, no food/water, drugs/poisons cease metabolizing) while senses remain active. First Aid -90%, Paramedic -70%, Medical Doctor -50% to detect the state. Willing feign-death can be canceled at will when attacked — only ends if the user chooses unless life is in jeopardy, then they revive without penalties and may surprise attackers. On unwilling targets (Morphus only): failed save vs magic 12+ stiffens muscles; P.S., P.P., Spd, attacks, combat bonuses, and skills reduced 20–80% by P.P.E. spent.

activationTiers.summary: Feign death on self or willing participant
Debilitate unwilling target: 5 P.P.E. per -20% penalty imposed (80% maximum)

ppe.notes: Three P.P.E. on self or willing feign death. Five P.P.E. per -20% penalty on unwilling victims (80% max — most victims barely move with 1–2 attacks).

limitations.otherLimitations: When feigning death willingly, effects cancel only when the user chooses — being stabbed does not automatically end the Talent unless they want to break the pretense.

ranges.summary: Touch or up to 20 feet (6.1 m) +5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Opponents: one minute per level. Self or willing feign death: one hour per level.

save.summary: Unwilling targets save vs magic 12 or higher.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Muscles stiffen; P.S., P.P., Spd, attacks per melee, combat bonuses, and skill performance reduced 20–80% based on P.P.E. spent.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Touch

duration.durationSummary: One hour per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Appears completely dead. Heartbeat and breathing stop, temperature cools. Uses 5% of normal oxygen, requires no food/water, ceases metabolizing drugs/poison. Still able to see, hear, and smell. Can be canceled at any time.

enhancement.summary: Every 5 P.P.E. imposes a -20% penalty, up to a maximum of 80%.
enhancement.stepDefinition: -20% penalty to physical stats and actions

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Touch or up to 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.durationSummary: One minute per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Reduces P.S., P.P., Spd, attacks per melee, combat bonuses, and skill performance by 20-80% based on P.P.E. spent.

prerequisites.summary: Level 4 or greater and at least one feature from the Undead, Pestilence, or Unnatural Limbs table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch or up to 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

activationTime.summary: Instant — 3 P.P.E. feign death; 5 P.P.E. per -20% debilitation step on unwilling targets.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.attacksPerMelee: Reduced 20-80%; at 80% most victims have only 1-2 attacks per melee
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Debilitate mode only: P.S., P.P., Spd, attacks per melee, combat bonuses, and skill performance reduced by 20-80% based on P.P.E. spent (5 P.P.E. per -20%, 80% maximum).

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Feign-death mode: suspended animation — uses 5% normal oxygen, requires no food or water, ceases metabolizing drugs and poison. Senses (sight, hearing, smell) remain active. First Aid -90%, Paramedic -70%, Medical Doctor -50% to detect the state.

statusEffects.name: Feign Death
statusEffects.summary: Appears completely dead — no heartbeat, breathing, or brain activity on modern medical equipment; core temperature matches surroundings.

statusEffects.name: Muscle Stiffness
statusEffects.summary: Debilitate mode: forced, difficult movement; attributes and combat performance reduced 20-80%.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: Willing feign-death user may cancel at any time when attacked; if life is in jeopardy, may immediately negate Rigor Mortis and fight or escape without penalties, likely surprising attackers.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Unwilling targets only (Debilitate mode).

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Muscles stiffen; P.S., P.P., Spd, attacks per melee, combat bonuses, and skill performance reduced 20-80% based on P.P.E. spent.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: No effect.
savingThrow.notes: Feign-death mode requires willing self or willing participant — no save. Being stabbed does not automatically end feign death unless the user chooses.

rules: Feign-death mode: ceases metabolizing drugs and poison in the body.
Feign-death mode: uses only 5% of normal oxygen required.

statusAlterations.summary: Feign death: stiff corpse appearance with suspended animation. Debilitate: rigor-mortis-like muscle stiffening impeding movement.

name: Seedling
description: Allows the user to plant a seed imbued with a fragment of their life essence. After 2D4 weeks it sprouts into a small tree reflecting their Morphus form. Growth stages grant remote senses, meld-and-hide refuge, a P.P.E. battery, lifespan extension, and a secret coma Hit Point buffer linked to the Life Tree.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E., one Hit Point, and one P.E. attribute point are permanently placed into the Seedling when it is planted. Communing with or hiding within a Seedling has no cost.

attributeBurn.summary: One Hit Point and one P.E. attribute point are permanently lost when the Seedling is planted.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only ONE seedling can be planted for the lifetime of the Nightbane. Supernatural appearance can be masked briefly (see P.P.E. costs). Communing with or hiding within a Seedling has no P.P.E. cost.

ranges.summary: Touch to plant a seedling.

duration.summary: The Seedling lives for decades or until destroyed. Lifespan extended 1D6×10+60 years while the tree remains alive and healthy. When the Nightbane dies, the tree lives another 1D6×10 years.

comaHitPointBuffer.summary: Provides 1D6+1 extra Hit Points per level of experience that are secretly added to the Nightbane's total when dropping below zero.
comaHitPointBuffer.durationWhenTriggered: Remains in a healing coma state until regenerated to 1D6+10 Hit Points above zero.

statusAlterations.summary: Lifespan extended by 1D6x10+60 years. +30% to save vs coma/death. Can breathe without air for 2 minutes per P.E. attribute point. Immune to carbon dioxide.

createdObjects.objectSummary: A magical tree tied to the creator's essence.

growthStages.growthStageSummary: Sprouts into a small sapling (1D4+4 feet tall).
growthStages.abilitiesAtStage: Nightbane can see, hear, and feel everything within a 20 foot (6.1 m) radius by concentrating for 1 minute.

Nightbane can meld with and hide inside the tree. Heals at double rate, needs no food/water, gains awareness in 200 ft radius. Tree acts as a P.P.E. battery containing 5D6+6 P.P.E. accessible up to 1 mile away.

growthStages.growthStageSummary: Doubles in size a final time.
growthStages.abilitiesAtStage: Gains full lifespan extension, coma/death bonuses, and secret Hit Point buffer.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one feature from the Angelic, Alien Shape, Mythical Creature, Plant Life, or Unnatural Limbs table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch to plant. Remote communion and P.P.E. draw work at any distance on Earth (communion requires 1 minute concentration). P.P.E. battery accessible up to 1 mile (1.6 km) away.

activationTime.summary: Planting: instant (5 P.P.E., 1 Hit Point, and 1 P.E. permanently invested). Communion: 1 minute concentration.

activationRequirements.summary: Only one Seedling per lifetime. Must plant in Morphus form.

powerModes.effectsDescription: After sprouting (2D4 weeks): spend 1 minute concentrating to see, hear, and feel everything within 20 feet (6.1 m) of the tree — any distance on Earth.

After 1D4+2 months: meld into the tree. Cannot be sensed or located by conventional or paranormal means. Double healing rate, no food/water needed, time inside counts as meditation for rest and P.P.E. recovery. Awareness within 200 feet (61 m).

bonuses.notes: Final growth stage: +30% to save vs coma/death regardless of circumstance or treatment.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Final stage: breathe without air for 2 minutes per P.E. point; immune to carbon dioxide. Secret 1D6+1 Hit Points per level buffer when dropping below zero Hit Points.

temporaryResourcePools.summary: Mature+ tree acts as a living P.P.E. battery (5D6+6 P.P.E.) drawable up to 1 mile (1.6 km) away.

endWhenThresholdsMet.notes: When Hit Points drop below zero, coma state regenerates using secret buffer Hit Points until 1D6+10 above zero, then awakens.

recoveryStateUntilThreshold.notes: Coma appears as death/dying but continues normal regeneration unless damage exceeds the buffer (then save vs coma/death applies).

healing.summary: While melded inside the mature tree: heal at double the normal rate.
healing.notes: If the tree is destroyed, all bonuses and abilities are lost; the Nightbane still lives out any extended lifespan already gained.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: +30% to save vs coma/death at final growth stage, regardless of circumstance or level of treatment.

rules: Final growth stage: immune to carbon dioxide.
Final growth stage: may breathe without air for 2 minutes per P.E. attribute point.

combatMechanics.notes: Secret Hit Point buffer: 1D6+1 per level added when dropping below zero. Coma regenerates until 1D6+10 Hit Points above zero unless buffer is exceeded. First Aid -90%, Paramedic -70%, Medical Doctor -50% to detect feign-death/suspended-animation state is N/A for tree meld — melded character cannot be sensed or located.

name: Shadow Swarm
description: Creates a horrific illusion of vermin swarming in shadows — mice, rats, frogs, snakes, spiders, beetles, cockroaches, flies, and similar crawlers nipping at heels just slower than the victim. Horror Factor 15 (average person), 12 (typical Nightbane), 17 (phobia of vermin, darkness, or shadows). The swarm will not enter brightly lit areas but circles just beyond light, corralling victims. Those caught or stepping in the swarm vanish into darkness for one melee round + 10 seconds per user level, then reappear unhurt. No damage — fear, corralling, and distraction.

ppe.notes: Eight P.P.E. to activate.

cannotAffect: Brightly lit areas (the swarm cannot enter light)
limitations.otherLimitations: Illusion affects everyone in the area who fails the Horror Factor save; user and spared allies are unaffected. Shooting into the swarm appears to kill vermin but has no real effect. Even after the illusion ends, many refuse to return for 1D6+4 melee rounds (half for heroes).

ranges.summary: Cast up to 200 feet (61 m) away; 30 foot (9.1 m) radius + 5% per level of experience.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) + 8 seconds per level of experience.

save.summary: Horror Factor 15 (average person), 12 (typical Nightbane), 17 (phobia of vermin, darkness, or shadows).
save.notes: Those who save and those deliberately spared do not see the swarming vermin.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Cast up to 200 feet (61 m) away.
rangeAndArea.areaOfEffectSummary: 30 foot (9.1 m) radius + 5% per level of experience.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Average person. Typical Nightbane: HF 12. Phobia of vermin, darkness, or shadows: HF 17.

statusAlterations.summary: Failed save: vermin swarm seems to lurk in every shadow. Victims caught or stepping in the swarm vanish into darkness — screams stop; witnesses may scream, weep, or faint. Vanished victims experience calm for ~10 subjective seconds, then reappear unhurt at the vanish point.

prerequisites.summary: Level 3 or greater and at least one feature from the Animal, Pestilence, or Undead table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Cast up to 200 feet (61 m) away; 30 foot (9.1 m) radius + 5% per level. User may exclude teammates or target specific individuals within the area.

activationTime.summary: 8 P.P.E. to activate.

activationRequirements.summary: User is unaffected and may control the illusion to spare teammates or affect only selected people within the radius.

swarmMechanics.movementSummary: Swarm moves just slower than victims, nipping at heels; circles beyond light without entering brightly lit areas.
swarmMechanics.notes: Shooting into the swarm appears to kill vermin but has no effect. Even successful HF saves may flee with panicked groups.

environmentalConstructs.summary: Shadow Swarm will not enter brightly lit areas — not even under a lamppost — but swarms and circles just beyond the edge of light, corralling victims.

statusEffects.name: Vanished into Shadows
statusEffects.summary: Caught victims vanish for one melee round + 10 seconds per user level; subjective experience ~10 seconds of calm, then reappear unhurt at the vanish location.
statusEffects.durationSummary: 1 melee round + 10 seconds per Talent user level

statusEffects.name: Horror Factor Swarm
statusEffects.summary: Illusion visible only to those who fail the Horror Factor save (or are not deliberately spared).

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Usual Horror Factor failure penalties apply. After the illusion ends, many refuse to return for 1D6+4 melee rounds to screw up courage (half duration for heroes).

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Sees and reacts to the vermin swarm; may be engulfed and vanished into shadows (1 melee round + 10 seconds per user level, unhurt on return).
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Does not see the swarm; may still flee with panicked groups.
savingThrow.notes: HF 12 for typical Nightbane; HF 17 for phobia of vermin, darkness, or shadows. No damage.

combatMechanics.notes: Used to corral opponents, chase bystanders away, discourage melee approach, distract, or cover escape.

name: Shadows of Despair
description: Manipulates the emotions of victims, plunging them into the depths of despair and hopelessness.

ppe.notes: Twelve P.P.E. per 1D4+1 melee rounds, or 120 P.P.E. for 1D4+2 days.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot make someone suicidal. Successful save grants 24-hour immunity.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Two targets by touch or one target up to 100 feet (30.5 m) away; line of sight required.

duration.durationSummary: 1D4+1 melee rounds per 12 P.P.E. spent, or 1D4+2 days if 120 P.P.E. is spent.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Magical, supernatural, and alien beings get +5 to save.

statusAlterations.summary: -6 on Perception Rolls, initiative, and save vs Horror Factor. -3 on saving throws and all other combat bonuses. -25% on skill rolls (-50% if life and death). Interrogators gain +20%. Remaining combat bonuses halved. Victim fatigues twice as fast and heals at half rate. 60% chance to flee/surrender at half HP unless a M.E. save of 16 is made.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Morphus feature from the Nightmare, Pestilence, Substance Abuse, or Victim table.

name: Share the Needle
description: Recreates drug addiction and withdrawal symptoms to debilitate an enemy, teach a lesson, or out of spite — or instantly sober someone up. User selects one symptom while in physical contact; only one affliction at a time. Supernatural or magical creatures suffer half penalties.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. per 1D6 minutes or 20 P.P.E. for 1D4x10 minutes. Only one symptom can be inflicted at a time.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only one symptom can be inflicted at a time. Penalties and effects are at half when applied to supernatural or magical creatures. Successful save: completely unaffected.

ranges.summary: Touch — physical contact with the intended victim while expending P.P.E.

duration.summary: 1D6 minutes per 5 P.P.E., or 1D4×10 minutes per 20 P.P.E.

save.summary: Save vs magic 15 or higher to resist a selected symptom. Sober Up: save vs magic 12 or higher to remain under the influence.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Selected withdrawal symptom applies for the paid duration.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Touch

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Withdrawal symptoms. Supernatural or magical creatures suffer half penalties on failure.
Sober Up mode only — target may resist and remain under the influence.

statusAlterations.summary: Anxiety/Restlessness/Wired: -20% to all skills, -1 to Perception, -3 to save vs Horror Factor.
Cold Sweats: Highly uncomfortable, -5% on all skills. Tracking victim by scent is +15%.
Dizziness: When moving faster than Spd 4 or fighting: -2 attacks, -4 to all combat rolls, -40% to skills. Balance-related skills are impossible.
Fatigue: Spd, attacks, and combat bonuses halved. -10% to skills. Fatigues twice as fast.
Headache: -1 to initiative and Perception, -20% to skills, -1 to all saving throws.
Muscle Cramps: P.S., P.P., Spd, and all combat bonuses reduced by half.
Nausea/Vomiting: Moving faster than half Spd or strenuous activity has a 0-75% chance per melee of causing vomiting. Vomiting uses all but one melee attack and costs initiative.
Tremors: P.P., Spd, and skill performance reduced by half. -6 to strike with ranged/thrown weapons.
Sober Up: Alleviates drug/alcohol highs within 1D4 melee rounds and reduces subsequent hangover/withdrawal symptoms by half.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one trait from the Substance Abuse or Victim table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch — physical contact required while expending P.P.E.

activationTime.summary: 5 P.P.E. per 1D6 minutes of one symptom, or 20 P.P.E. for 1D4×10 minutes.

activationRequirements.summary: Select exactly one symptom per use; no multiple afflictions.

powerModes.effectsDescription: -20% all skills, -1 Perception, -3 save vs Horror Factor.
Highly uncomfortable; -5% all skills. Clothes soaked within minutes. Tracking by scent +15%.
Relief when lying down. Moving faster than Spd 4 or fighting: -2 attacks, -4 all combat rolls, -40% skills; balance skills impossible; falls to ground.
Spd, attacks, and combat bonuses halved; -10% skills; fatigues twice as fast.
-1 initiative and Perception, -20% skills, -1 all saving throws.
P.S., P.P., Spd, and all combat bonuses reduced by half.
Moving faster than half Spd, being jostled, or strenuous activity: 0–75% chance per melee of vomiting. Vomiting uses all but one attack and costs initiative.
P.P. and Spd halved; skill performance halved; -6 strike with ranged/thrown weapons.
Alleviates drug/alcohol highs within 1D4 melee rounds; reduces subsequent hangover/withdrawal symptoms by half. Target may save vs magic 12+ to remain under the influence.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Penalties vary by selected symptom (see powerModes and statusAlterations). Supernatural or magical creatures suffer half penalties.

statusEffects.name: Withdrawal Symptom
statusEffects.summary: One selected addiction/withdrawal symptom for the paid duration.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Selected symptom applies for 1D6 minutes (5 P.P.E.) or 1D4×10 minutes (20 P.P.E.).
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Completely unaffected.
savingThrow.notes: Sober Up mode uses save vs magic 12 to resist sobriety. Half penalties on supernatural/magical creatures.

name: Sideshow
description: Grants preternatural acrobat/gymnast movement in and out of combat — flipping and tumbling like a kung-fu movie or circus performer. Grants equivalent skills at 86% and combat bonuses for mobile fighting.

enhancement.summary: 6 P.P.E. per additional minute of effect.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. to activate; 6 P.P.E. to sustain each additional minute.

ranges.summary: Self

duration.summary: One minute per level of experience.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Self

statusAlterations.summary: +2 to parry and dodge, +3 to roll with punch/fall/impact. No strike penalties for attacking on the move. +2 to strike with kick attacks, +2 to disarm.

prerequisites.summary: Level 2 or greater and at least one Morphus feature from the Clown, Athlete, or Disproportion table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self only.

activationTime.summary: 10 P.P.E. to activate; 6 P.P.E. per additional minute sustained.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +2 strike with kick attacks only; +2 to disarm; +3 to roll with punch/fall/impact. No strike penalties applied for attacking on the move.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Moves with preternatural acrobatic grace — flipping and tumbling around opponents in and out of combat.
mobilityBlocks.notes: Equivalent acrobat/gymnast skills at 86% for the duration.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Grants equivalent skills at 86%: Back Flip, Climb Rope, Sense of Balance, Walk Tightrope/High-Wire, Work Parallel Bars & Rings.

combatMechanics.notes: No strike penalties for attacking on the move. +2 strike with kicks; +2 disarm; +3 roll with punch/fall/impact; +2 parry and dodge.

name: Spelljack
description: Hijacks control of a spell cast by someone near the Nightbane or on an area, object, or person within range. Once in control, the Nightbane may immediately cancel the spell, keep it in effect, reduce its duration or range/area, or seize control of those affected. Instant-duration spells require winning an initiative contest vs the caster (Nightbane adds initiative bonus); if the mage wins, the spell resolves normally. Living Spells that fail save vs 15 fall under complete control for 1D4 minutes per user level — likely hostile when control ends.

baseActivation.summary: One quarter the P.P.E. cost of the spell being jacked (rounded up)

ppe.notes: Twenty P.P.E. to acquire permanently. Hijack cost scales with the spell's P.P.E. cost.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only spell magic — not rituals, wards, circles, runes, or magic weapons. Instant-duration spells require winning initiative vs the caster (Nightbane adds initiative bonus); if the mage wins, the spell resolves with intended range, duration, level, and effects unchanged.

ranges.summary: Caster or spell target within 25 feet (7.6 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience

duration.summary: Living Spell control lasts 1D4 minutes per user level.

save.summary: Living Spells save vs 15 or are Spelljacked for 1D4 minutes per level.

prerequisites.summary: At least one feature from the Fantasy or Infernal table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Caster or spell target within 25 feet (7.6 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: 25 feet (7.6 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level to the caster or spell target.

activationTime.summary: One quarter the jacked spell's P.P.E. cost (rounded up). Instant spells: opposed initiative vs caster.

activationRequirements.summary: Spell magic only — rituals, wards, circles, runes, and magic weapons are unaffected.
activationRequirements.circumstance: Target must be an active spell cast within range

powerModes.effectsDescription: Immediately ends the hijacked spell.
Keep the spell in effect under the Nightbane's control.
Reduce the hijacked spell's duration or range/area of effect.
Take control of those affected by the hijacked spell.
Living Spells that fail save vs 15 obey any command for 1D4 minutes per user level.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Living Spells only. Failed save: complete control for 1D4 minutes per user level.

combatMechanics.notes: Instant-duration spells: opposed initiative roll vs caster (Nightbane adds initiative bonus). Mage wins — spell resolves normally. Nightbane wins — seizes control before the spell takes effect.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Living Spell forced to obey any command for 1D4 minutes per user level.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Living Spell resists Spelljack.
savingThrow.notes: Living Spells only. After control ends, the Living Spell is likely very threatened and/or angry with the user.

statusAlterations.summary: Hijacked spell magic bends to the Nightbane's will — cancelled, sustained, weakened, or turned against its original purpose.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Living Spells under control obey commands for 1D4 minutes per user level, then are likely hostile toward the Spelljacker.

name: Summoner
description: Summons loyal demonic minions from magical portals — Sub-Demons, Lesser Demons, or a Greater Demon — that obey to the point of self-sacrifice, then vanish when the duration ends. Portal and demon appearance vary by summoner; G.M. may substitute Red Flame Demons or other Palladium demons.

activationTiers.summary: One Sub-Demon (up to three may be summoned)
One Lesser Demon (up to two may be summoned)

enhancement.summary: Strength (+5 Supernatural P.S. and +50 S.D.C.), Flight, Stealth, Biting Jaws, Armament, or Firepower — 5 P.P.E. per demon per enhancement, maximum three enhancements.
enhancement.stepDefinition: One enhancement on one summoned demon

ppe.notes: Twenty P.P.E. per Sub-Demon, 40 per Lesser Demon, 80 per Greater Demon. Each enhancement adds 5 P.P.E. per demon (limit three enhancements total per summoning).

limitations.otherLimitations: Up to three Sub-Demons, two Lesser Demons, or one Greater Demon per activation. Minions are completely loyal for the duration, then disappear. Maximum three enhancements total per summoning (5 P.P.E. per demon per enhancement).

ranges.summary: Portals open up to 30 feet (9.1 m) away; summoned demons may travel up to 1,000 feet (305 m) per user level.

duration.summary: Sub-Demons: 40 minutes. Lesser Demons: 20 minutes. Greater Demon: 10 minutes.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Portals open up to 30 feet (9.1 m) away. Demons can travel up to 1,000 feet (305 m) away per level of experience.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one Infernal, Fantasy, or Nightmare Morphus trait.

rangeMechanics.summary: Portals up to 30 feet (9.1 m) from the user. Summoned demons may operate up to 1,000 feet (305 m) away per user level.

activationTime.summary: Portals appear on activation; demons emerge within 1D4 seconds.

powerModes.effectsDescription: 100 S.D.C., 20 HP, A.R. 11, P.S. 20, P.P. 16, P.E. 14, Spd 24, HF 10. Claw +1D6 supernatural punch. 3 attacks, +1 combat, +2 damage, +1 saves. Up to 3 per activation. Duration 40 minutes.
150 S.D.C., 30 HP, A.R. 12, M.E. 16, P.S. 25, P.P. 18, P.E. 18, Spd 20, HF 12. Claw +1D6+3 supernatural punch. 4 attacks, +2 combat, +6 damage, +2 saves. Up to 2 per activation. Duration 20 minutes.

250 S.D.C., 50 HP, A.R. 13, I.Q./M.E. 18, M.A. 20, P.S. 30, P.P. 20, P.E. 22, Spd 16, HF 14. Claw +2D6 supernatural punch. 6 attacks, +4 combat, +10 damage, +3 saves. One per activation. Duration 10 minutes.

createdObjects.name: Demonic Minion
createdObjects.summary: Loyal summoned demon(s) from portals. Optional enhancements (5 P.P.E. per demon each, max 3 total): Strength (+5 S.P.S., +50 S.D.C.), Flight (Spd 1D4×10+20, +2 dodge in flight), Stealth (Prowl 65%, Shadow Meld at will), Biting Jaws (bite half S.P.S. punch +1 attack), Armament (3D6 weapon +2 strike/parry), Firepower (Fire Ball 5D6 or breath fire 5D6 at 50 ft, +3 strike).

passengerCompanionCapacity.summary: Up to 3 Sub-Demons, 2 Lesser Demons, or 1 Greater Demon per activation.
passengerCompanionCapacity.notes: Minions are completely loyal to self-sacrifice and carry out orders for the duration.

combatMechanics.notes: Demons emerge within 1D4 seconds of portal opening. G.M. may use Red Flame Demons (Nightlands) or demons from other Palladium books instead of quick-stats.

statusAlterations.summary: Portals and demon appearance vary by summoner — hellish insectoids from black spheres, flaming demons from earth cracks, etc.

name: Swarm Self
description: Separates the Nightbane's body into 2D4×10 tiny animals of the same type as the character's Morphus (birds, rats, spiders, wasps, etc.). The user controls the swarm and senses everything they see or do. Each swarmling has 5 S.D.C. and 1 Hit Point; groups of 10+ strike for 5D6 per melee. HP damage to the swarm carries over when the Nightbane reforms; if all swarmlings are destroyed, the Nightbane dies.

prerequisites.summary: At least one characteristic from the Animal or Insect Form Table. Swarm creatures are small versions of the associated animal (avian→birds, rodent→rats/mice, arachnid→spiders/scorpions, insect→wasps/bees/flies, etc.).

limitations.otherLimitations: Swarmlings do not regenerate damage. At least two swarmlings required to rebuild; most retreat and reform when reduced to 10–15. If all swarmlings are destroyed, the Nightbane is slain. Once transformed, swarm range is unrestricted.

enhancement.summary: An additional 10 P.P.E. for each additional two minutes.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. for the first two minutes and an additional 10 P.P.E. for each additional two minutes.

ranges.summary: Self; once a swarm, the range is unrestricted.

duration.summary: Two minutes

swarmMechanics.collectivePoolSummary: Each swarm member has 5 S.D.C. and 1 Hit Point. A swarm of 10 critters will have 50 S.D.C. and 10 Hit Points. Attacks on the swarm have no modifiers to hit.

reversionDamageCarryover.summary: The hit point (not S.D.C.) damage taken by the swarm carries over to the Nightbane's hit points after the swarm is destroyed or recalled.

swarmMechanics.notes: In combat, they do 1 point of S.D.C. damage individually. If the swarm takes more than half its S.D.C. damage, damage is reduced to 2D6. When all S.D.C. is depleted, only two swarmlings remain. At least two swarmlings must be present for the Nightbane to rebuild himself; most retreat and reform when reduced to 10 or 15. If all are destroyed, the Nightbane is slain. Individual swarmlings: called shot at -5 to hit; same combat bonuses as the Nightbane.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self transformation; once a swarm, range is unrestricted.

activationTime.summary: 10 P.P.E. for first two minutes; +10 P.P.E. per additional two minutes.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: 1 S.D.C. individually; 5D6 per group of 10+ (several groups may attack different targets). Half S.D.C. lost: group damage drops to 2D6.
attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Group of 10 auto-swarm in 3–5 seconds (one melee action); damage at end of melee round.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Individual swarmlings use the Nightbane's combat bonuses but require a called shot at -5 to hit. Attacks on the swarm have no to-hit modifiers.

damage.summary: Individual: 1 S.D.C. Group of 10+: 5D6 at end of melee (2D6 if swarm lost more than half S.D.C.). Two swarmlings remain when all S.D.C. depleted.

combatMechanics.notes: Swarm of 10 = 50 S.D.C. and 10 HP collectively. Hit Point (not S.D.C.) damage carries over to the Nightbane on recall or destruction.

endWhenThresholdsMet.notes: At least two swarmlings must remain to rebuild. Most swarms retreat and reform when reduced to 10–15 members.

recoveryStateUntilThreshold.notes: If all swarmlings are destroyed, the Nightbane is slain. User must return to normal Morphus form to limp away; swarmlings do not regenerate damage.

statusAlterations.summary: Body separates into 2D4×10 tiny animals; user aware of all swarm senses. Reform requires ≥2 surviving swarmlings.

name: The Taint
description: Anything the Nightbane touches decays — metal rusts, cloth tatters, wood and plaster crack, rubber rots, plants wither. Living victims feel chilling necrotic pain: 1D6 damage per 2 P.P.E. spent (9 P.P.E. base +2 P.P.E. per additional 1D6, up to 6 P.P.E./3D6 per level). Magical necrosis halves natural recovery rate and healing effectiveness; medical and repair skills suffer -20%. Touch attack — dodgeable; parrying objects or weapons take the damage. Natural A.R. does not protect supernatural targets. Does 30% more damage to Night Princes and Nightlords.

enhancement.summary: +2 P.P.E. per additional 1D6 damage (up to 6 P.P.E. / 3D6 per level of experience).

ppe.notes: Nine P.P.E. to activate plus 2 P.P.E. per 1D6 damage. Maximum 6 P.P.E. (3D6) per level on damage. P.P.E. is spent even if the attack misses.

limitations.otherLimitations: Touch attack — can be dodged or parried (parrying object takes damage). Successful save vs magic: one-third damage. Natural A.R. does not protect supernatural targets. Does 30% more damage to Night Princes and Nightlords. +2 Horror Factor if the attack continues. Victims below zero Hit Points are -10% to save vs coma/death. P.P.E. is spent even if the attack misses.

ranges.summary: Touch

duration.summary: Taint touch lasts one melee round per activation.

save.summary: Save vs magic; on success suffer only one-third damage.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Undead or Victim table characteristic.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch attack.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Touch.

activationTime.summary: 9 P.P.E. base activation + 2 P.P.E. per additional 1D6 damage (max 6 P.P.E. / 3D6 per level). P.P.E. spent even on a miss.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: 1D6 per 2 P.P.E. spent on damage (9 P.P.E. activation + up to 6 P.P.E. extra per level).
attacksAndDamage.notes: Can be dodged or parried. Parrying object or weapon takes the damage instead of the defender.

offensiveDamage.summary: 1D6 per 2 P.P.E. spent (e.g. 11 P.P.E. total = 1D6 activation + 1D6 from +2 P.P.E.).

bonusDamageVsNightlandsDenizens.summary: 30% more damage to Night Princes and Nightlords.

objectsAffected.summary: Inanimate matter touched decays — metal rusts, cloth tatters, wood/plaster cracks, rubber rots, plants wither. Repair skills on damaged objects suffer -20%.

healing.summary: Taint necrosis: living beings recover S.D.C. and Hit Points at half rate and take twice as long. Medical treatment at -20%; magical and psionic healing half as effective.
healing.notes: Applies to damage from this Talent until fully recovered.

rules: Natural A.R. does not protect supernatural beings (Nightbane, Wampyrs, etc.) — if touched, they take damage.
Victims dropped below zero Hit Points by The Taint are -10% to save vs coma/death.
Successful save vs magic: suffer only one-third damage.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Save vs magic; on success suffer only one-third damage.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Medical skills to treat Taint damage at -20%. Object repair skills at -20%.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Full necrotic damage and decay effects.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: One-third damage only; necrosis/healing penalties may still apply per G.M.
savingThrow.notes: Touch attack — dodge or parry (parrying item takes damage). Continuing attacks add +2 to attacker's Horror Factor. P.P.E. spent even if the attack misses.

damage.summary: 1D6 per 2 P.P.E. spent on damage, plus 30% vs Night Princes and Nightlords.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: P.P.E. is spent even if the touch attack fails to connect.

statusAlterations.summary: Touched matter visibly decays. Victims feel dark energy like ice water — chilling cold and pain. Continued attacks add +2 Horror Factor to the attacker.

name: The Ties that Bind
description: Materializes ensnaring chains, razor wire, barbed tentacles, or hooks that shoot out to strike, entangle, constrict, lift, and torture. Each tendril has supernatural P.S. 16 (+3 strike/entangle; user's strike bonuses do not apply). Agony pulses travel pain down ensnared victims.

enhancement.summary: +1 P.P.E. per additional point of tendril P.S. (base 16, maximum 24). Agony pulses cost 2 P.P.E. per tendril used.

ppe.notes: Fifteen P.P.E. per tendril created, plus 1 P.P.E. per additional point of tendril P.S. (maximum 24). Each agony pulse costs 2 P.P.E. per tendril.

limitations.otherLimitations: Targets must be visible and within range. Victims with Supernatural P.S. equal to or greater than the combined entangling tendril strength may break free (two melee actions; 2D6 damage per tendril whether freed or not). Tendrils severed by 26 S.D.C. in one attack; vulnerable only to spells, psionics, Talents, magical weapons, and Supernatural beings.

ranges.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute (4 melee rounds) per level of experience.

save.summary: Agony pulse: save vs pain 12 or better (P.E. bonuses apply). -2 to save per additional simultaneous tendril beyond the first.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Lose two attacks; cannot use spells, psionics, Talents, skills, or do anything but scream for the melee round.
save.effectsOnSuccessfulSave: Lose one melee attack; -1 to save against the next agony pulse.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: Tendril strike does 4D6 damage (6D6 against Nightlords/minions). Constriction does half damage (2D6) and cannot be defended against. Tendrils have P.S. 16 and +3 to strike/entangle.
attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Breaking free takes two melee actions.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Agony pulse only (P.E. bonuses apply). -2 to save for each tendril beyond the first when pulsing simultaneously.

statusAlterations.summary: One tendril imposes -2 to subsequent combat rolls. Two tendrils double this to -4. Three or more prevent any defensive action except parrying with a free limb at -6.
Throat constriction (requires Called Shot 16+ unmodified): Cuts off airway. Unconsciousness in 1D6+4 melees, coma after 1D4+2 mins, death in 2D4 mins.
Agony Pulse (Failed Save): Victim loses two attacks and cannot use spells, psionics, skills, or do anything but scream for the melee round.
Agony Pulse (Successful Save): Victim loses one melee attack and is -1 to save against the next agony pulse.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one feature from the Infernal, Nightmare, or Stigmata table.

rangeMechanics.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level; visible targets within range.

activationTime.summary: 15 P.P.E. per tendril + 1 P.P.E. per additional tendril P.S. point (base 16, max 24). Agony pulse: 2 P.P.E. per tendril.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Tendril strike: 4D6 damage (6D6 vs Nightlords/minions/Nightlands denizens). +3 to strike; user's usual strike bonuses do not apply.
+3 to entangle. One tendril: -2 subsequent combat rolls. Two tendrils: -4. Three or more: no defense except parry with free limb at -6. May lift target into the air.
Half damage (2D6), cannot be defended against. Vs armored targets, half damage to armor and half to S.D.C./HP.
Called Shot 16+ unmodified to entangle throat. Unconsciousness in 1D6+4 melees (mortals), coma after 1D4+2 minutes, death after additional 2D4 minutes.

ppe.notes: 2 P.P.E. per tendril used.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Burning/stabbing pain along contact point. Failed save vs pain 12 (P.E. only): lose 2 attacks, can only scream. Success: lose 1 attack, -1 vs next pulse. -2 save per extra simultaneous tendril.

bonusDamageVsNightlandsDenizens.summary: 6D6 vs Nightlords, their demonic minions, and all Denizens of the Nightlands.
bonusDamageVsNightlandsDenizens.notes: Strike damage is 6D6 instead of 4D6 against Nightlands targets.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.strike: -2 with one entangling tendril; -4 with two; parry only at -6 with three or more
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Agony pulse failure: lose two attacks and can only scream for the melee round.

holdsAndEscapes.summary: Break free if Supernatural P.S. equals or exceeds combined entangling tendril strength.
holdsAndEscapes.notes: Breaking free inflicts 2D6 damage per tendril whether successful or not. Tendrils severed by 26 S.D.C. in a single attack.

rules: Tendrils may be severed by 26 S.D.C. damage in a single attack.
Tendrils are only vulnerable to spells, psionics, Talents, magical weapons, and Supernatural beings (including Nightbane S.P.S., claws, and bites).

damage.summary: Strike: 4D6 (6D6 vs Nightlords/minions/Nightlands denizens). Constriction: 2D6, undefendable.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Agony pulse: lose two attacks; cannot act except scream for the melee round.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: Lose one melee attack; -1 to save against the next agony pulse.
