savingThrow.notes: -2 to save per additional simultaneous tendril. Tendril P.S. 16 base (+1 P.P.E. per point, max 24).

statusEffects.name: Entangled
statusEffects.summary: Held in place by one or more tendrils; escalating combat penalties and vulnerability to constriction/agony.

name: Too Much Stereo
description: Turns the Nightbane into a stadium-quality sound system projecting voice or music at up to 150 decibels — clearly heard up to 2 miles (3.2 km) outdoors. Nearby objects rattle and vibrate; music within 500 feet (152.4 m) debilitates the unprotected. Once per melee round the user may also fire a directed sonic blast that destroys fragile objects and knocks victims down. Highly likely to attract authorities, NSB agents, and Nightlord minions.

activationTiers.summary: Directed sonic blast (once per melee round, in addition to amplification)

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. to amplify voice, 10 P.P.E. to blare music. Each directed sonic blast requires an additional 5 P.P.E. and can only be fired once per melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: Targets may not be selected individually for AoE effects; everyone within range is affected. May create confusion, interfere with communications, and serve as a distraction. Highly likely to attract authorities, NSB agents, and supernatural minions of the Nightlords.

ranges.summary: Voice/music heard up to 2 miles (3.2 km) outdoors. Music debilitation: 500 foot (152.4 m) radius. Sonic blast column: 600 feet (183 m); cone: 300 feet (91.4 m).

duration.summary: Sound amplification up to one minute per level of experience. Sonic blast once per melee round.

areaOfEffect.summary: Music debilitating effects: 500 foot (152.4 m) radius. Sonic blast: 6 foot (1.8 m) diameter column or 45–90° cone.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Voice/music clearly heard up to 2 miles (3.2 km) outdoors.
rangeAndArea.areaOfEffectSummary: 500 foot (152.4 m) radius for music penalties. Sonic column 600 feet (183 m) × 6 feet (1.8 m); cone 300 feet (91.4 m), 45–90° arc.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Clearly heard up to 2 miles (3.2 km) away outdoors.

duration.durationSummary: Up to one minute per level of experience.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Clearly heard up to 2 miles (3.2 km) away outdoors.
rangeAndArea.areaOfEffectSummary: 500 feet (152.4 m) radius for debilitating effects.

duration.durationSummary: Up to one minute per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Without hearing protection: Must shout to communicate (5 ft range max), -3 on Perception Rolls (-6 if requiring hearing), -2 on initiative, -2 to parry and disarm, -4 to dodge, and -15% on skill performance.

attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Can only be fired once per melee round, and counts as one melee attack.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Column-shaped (600 feet / 183 m long, 6 feet / 1.8 m in diameter) OR Cone-shaped (300 feet / 91.4 m long, 45-90 degree arc).

statusAlterations.summary: Knocks victims off their feet, causes loss of initiative, and loss of two melee attacks.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one Morphus feature from the Artisan, Hobbyist, or Biomechanical table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Outdoor audibility 2 miles (3.2 km). Music penalties within 500 feet (152.4 m). Sonic column 600 feet (183 m) × 6 feet (1.8 m); cone 300 feet (91.4 m), 45–90° arc.
rangeMechanics.notes: Cone blast has half column range (300 feet / 91.4 m).

activationTime.summary: 2 P.P.E. amplify voice, 10 P.P.E. blare music; +5 P.P.E. per sonic blast (once per melee, counts as one attack).

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: While music blares without hearing protection: must shout to communicate (5 ft / 1.5 m range), -2 to disarm, -15% skill performance. Sonic blast: knocked off feet, lose initiative and two melee attacks.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: Sonic blast: 2D6 damage; knocks victims off their feet; lose initiative and two melee attacks.
attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Once per melee round; counts as one of the Nightbane's melee attacks.

damage.summary: Directed sonic blast: 2D6 damage. Knocked off feet; lose initiative and two melee attacks. Destroys fragile objects.

combatMechanics.notes: Sonic blast may be column (600 ft / 183 m, 6 ft / 1.8 m diameter) or cone (300 ft / 91.4 m, 45–90° arc). Cannot select individual targets in AoE.

statusAlterations.summary: Music (500 ft radius, no hearing protection): shout-only communication (5 ft range), -3 Perception (-6 hearing-based), -2 initiative, -2 parry/disarm, -4 dodge, -15% skills.
Sonic blast: knocked off feet; lose initiative and two melee attacks for the melee round.

name: True Reflection
description: Temporarily imbues a mirror of any size with supernatural power. Shows the true face of anyone reflected — shape-changers in true form, Nightbane in Facade as Morphus (Facade not revealed when the viewer is in Morphus), illusions and metamorphosis magic broken, possession shown with a ghostly possessor. Gauges true alignment with alignment-specific psychological reactions. Vampires cast no reflection. Nightlords and Night Princes are revealed but not psychologically affected. Usable covertly (compact mirror) or overtly (mirrored ballroom wall).

activationTiers.summary: Massive mirror (wall or ceiling up to 100 feet / 30.5 m)

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. for a pocket mirror, 10 for medium, 15 for full-body, 20 for massive mirrors.

limitations.otherLimitations: Reveals true form, alignment, and possession. Vampires cast no reflection. Nightlords and Night Princes are revealed but not psychologically affected.

ranges.summary: Touch the mirror, effective up to 50 feet (15.2 m) away

duration.summary: Five minutes.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Artificial Appearance or Unusual Facial Feature characteristic.

rangeMechanics.summary: Touch the mirror to imbue it; reflections are effective up to 50 feet (15.2 m) away.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Touch to activate; effective up to 50 feet (15.2 m).

activationTime.summary: 5–20 P.P.E. by mirror size (see activation tiers).

powerModes.effectsDescription: Small pocket mirror — ideal for covert screening.
Head to half-torso size.
Full-body and larger mirrors.
Wall or ceiling mirror up to 100 feet (30.5 m) long.

reflectionMechanics.triggerCondition: Anyone reflected in an imbued mirror within range.
reflectionMechanics.notes: Reveals true form, alignment, and possession. Shape-changers shown truly; Nightbane in Facade shown in Morphus. Vampires cast no reflection. Nightlords and Night Princes revealed but not psychologically affected. Supernaturally evil creatures revealed, not affected. Humans, Wampyrs, Guardians, awakened Doppelgangers, and Nightbane are fully affected regardless of alignment.

alignmentGatedEffects.summary: Handsome, inspiring reflection (Principled virtually glow). First viewing ever: +2 save vs Horror Factor and magic for 1D4 days (non-repeatable).
Generally good-looking with unsightly blemishes. +1 save vs Horror Factor for 1D4 days.
True face with every blemish, scar, and imperfection greatly enhanced — opportunity for alignment introspection.
Twisted monstrous visage. Save vs Horror Factor 14 or recoil: lose attack and initiative, flee witnesses; 1D6 minutes shame (every viewing); first viewing only — -2 initiative for 1D4 days.
Monstrous reflection with nobility and redemption spark. Save vs Horror Factor 10 or suffer Diabolic/Miscreant effects.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Diabolic and Miscreant viewers only. Failure: recoil, lose attack/initiative, flee.
Aberrant viewers only. Failure: same effects as Diabolic/Miscreant.

bonusesOnAlliedTargets.notes: Principled/Scrupulous (first viewing only): +2 save vs Horror Factor and magic for 1D4 days. Unprincipled: +1 save vs Horror Factor for 1D4 days.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Diabolic/Miscreant failed HF 14: lose attack and initiative, flee. First viewing: -2 initiative for 1D4 days. Fear/shame lasts 1D6 minutes per viewing.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Diabolic/Miscreant: recoil, lose attack and initiative, flee anyone who may have seen. Aberrant uses HF 10 for same failure effects.
savingThrow.notes: Supernaturally evil creatures and Nightlords/Night Princes are revealed but not psychologically affected. Talent may prompt alignment shifts toward virtue (Aberrant/Anarchist especially).

objectsAffected.summary: Any mirror imbued for five minutes — pocket compact to 100-foot (30.5 m) wall or ceiling mirror.

statusAlterations.summary: Mirror shows true face, alignment, possession (ghostly possessor overlay), and breaks illusions/metamorphosis. Vampires appear reflectionless.

notes: Lightbringers use this Talent to screen recruits. Covert use: compact while 'fixing makeup.' Overt use: mirrored ballroom wall.

name: Urban Golem
description: Quickly cobbles together a dull-witted Golem-like automaton from man-made materials (brick, steel, glass, trash, etc.) to serve as combat backup. Appearance and durability depend on available materials; the construct persists without the creator nearby but only acts when commanded — otherwise it stands in place swatting at anyone in range.

activationTiers.summary: Lightweight structural metal or metal scrap

ppe.notes: Activation cost varies by man-made materials on hand.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only works on man-made or processed materials (cut lumber, broken furniture, bricks, cinder block, engine blocks, scrap metal, etc.). Only one automaton created and controlled. Nightbane loses all but two attacks per melee round while controlling it. Without commands, it stands in place swatting at whoever comes within range. Never travels beyond 600 feet (183 m) from the creator.

ranges.summary: Created up to 50 feet (15.2 m) away; commanded up to 600 feet (183 m) away.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of experience.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Created up to 50 feet (15.2 m) away. Commanded up to 600 feet (183 m) away; never goes farther.

environmentalMaterialScaling.sdcOrHpSummary: 2D4x10+20 S.D.C., Natural A.R. 8. Fire-based attacks ignore A.R. and do 50% more damage.

environmentalMaterialScaling.materialSummary: Lightweight Structural Metal or Metal Scrap

createdObjects.notes: Attributes: I.Q. 5, M.E. 6, M.A. 6, P.S. 22 (Supernatural), P.P. 10, P.E. 10, P.B. 4, Spd 14. 4 attacks/melee, +2 strike/parry/dodge, +3 roll with impact. 3D6 punch, 6D6 power punch, 2D6 bite, 4D6 claws. Horror Factor 1D4+9. 7-10 feet tall, 3 tons. Immune to poisons, illusions, mind control, air/food requirements, and HF.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one feature from the Artificial Appearance, Fantasy, Gear-Head, or Mineral table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Create within 50 feet (15.2 m); command up to 600 feet (183 m). Automaton never exceeds command range.

activationTime.summary: 20–150 P.P.E. depending on materials on hand.

powerModes.effectsDescription: 2D4×10+20 S.D.C., Natural A.R. 8. Fire-based attacks ignore A.R. and do 50% more damage.
2D6×10+40 S.D.C., Natural A.R. 10.
1D4×100+60 S.D.C., Natural A.R. 13.
1D6×100+80 S.D.C., Natural A.R. 15.
2D4×100+120 S.D.C., Natural A.R. 16.

passengerCompanionCapacity.summary: One Urban Golem automaton per activation.
passengerCompanionCapacity.notes: Too dull-witted for complex orders; great combat muscle. Continues to exist if creator is not nearby but only acts when commanded.

penaltiesOnCaster.attacksPerMelee: Loses all but two attacks per melee round while controlling the automaton.
penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Without commands the golem stands in place and swats at anyone within range.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: 3D6 supernatural punch, 6D6 power punch (2 attacks), 2D6 bite, 4D6 claws (if applicable). +2 strike/parry/dodge, +3 roll with impact. 4 attacks per melee.
attacksAndDamage.notes: Horror Factor 1D4+9. 7–10 feet tall, up to 3 tons.

damage.summary: Punch 3D6 (supernatural P.S.), power punch 6D6 (costs two melee attacks), bite 2D6, claws 4D6.

combatMechanics.notes: I.Q. 5, M.E. 6, M.A. 6, P.S. 22 (supernatural), P.P. 10, P.E. 10, P.B. 4, Spd 14. Immune to poisons, illusions/mind control, and Horror Factor. No food, water, air, or rest required. Sinks like a rock in deep water.

statusAlterations.summary: Appearance varies by creator's will and available materials — cobbled urban construct 7–10 feet (2.1–3 m) tall, up to 3 tons.

name: Vicious Cycle
description: Represents the tragic irony of abuse and neglect — the victim becoming the abuser. When engaged, the Nightbane becomes more physically imposing and aggressive: P.S. and S.D.C. boosted 20%, +1 attack per melee round. Morphus only; cannot affect Facade.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. to activate. Boosts P.S. and S.D.C. by 20% and grants +1 attack per melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot affect the Facade form.

ranges.summary: Self

duration.summary: One melee round per level of experience.

prerequisites.summary: One or more traits from the Victim or Disproportion table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self only.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Self.

activationTime.summary: 5 P.P.E. to activate.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +20% P.S. and +20% S.D.C. for the duration.

temporaryAbilitiesGranted.notes: Morphus form only — does not affect Facade.

combatMechanics.notes: User becomes more physically imposing and aggressive for one melee round per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Morphus form grows more imposing and aggressive — victim-turned-abuser surge of physical power.

name: Voice of the Beast
description: Communicate with animals matching the Nightbane's Morphus animal traits. Method chosen at acquisition: telepathy via eye contact, or species-appropriate vocalization (hissing, growling, buzzing, etc.). Cannot command animals — may ask questions or request help; the animal treats the Nightbane as a friendly same-species member but decides whether to help (G.M. discretion). Animal intelligence limits answers to simple questions within their nature and experience.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. to activate. Communication method (telepathy via eye contact or species-appropriate vocalization) is chosen when the talent is first acquired.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot command animals — only ask questions or request help (G.M. discretion). Deactivates if the Nightbane speaks to anyone other than the animal. Animals answer only simple questions within their nature and intelligence.

ranges.summary: Communication with animals of the same type as the Nightbane's Morphus traits

duration.summary: Remains active while communicating with the animal only; must reactivate after speaking to others.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Animal characteristic.

activationRequirements.summary: Communication method (telepathy via eye contact or species vocalization) must be chosen when the Talent is first acquired.

rangeMechanics.summary: Same animal type as the Nightbane's Morphus traits — line of sight or hearing as appropriate to the chosen method.
rangeMechanics.notes: Telepathy requires eye contact; vocalization uses species-appropriate sounds.

activationTime.summary: 5 P.P.E. to activate.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Communicate through eye contact, similar to Telepathy. Chosen permanently at acquisition.
Communicate through hissing, growling, buzzing, or other species-appropriate sounds. Chosen permanently at acquisition.

combatMechanics.notes: Cannot command animals. Animals answer only simple questions within their nature and intelligence (G.M. discretion).

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Talent deactivates if the Nightbane speaks to anyone other than the animal — must reactivate (5 P.P.E.) to resume beast communication.

statusAlterations.summary: Animals of the matching type consider the Nightbane a friendly member of their species while the Talent is active.

notes: Animals retain animal-level intelligence and perspective — not a command power.

name: War Cry
description: Unleashes a supernatural howl — from an angry beast's bellow to shouted patron-spirit invocations — powerful enough to demoralize and cow enemies who hear it. Raises the user's Horror Factor per P.P.E. spent; failed saves scatter a portion of foes and cripple the rest with combat penalties.

enhancement.summary: Five P.P.E. per each +1 bonus to the Talent user's Horror Factor and the volume and ferocity of the War Cry.

ppe.notes: Maximum save imposed is 18; boosting Horror Factor beyond 18 counteracts victims' save bonuses on a 1:1 basis.

limitations.otherLimitations: Each use consumes half of the Nightbane's attacks for that melee round. Area may be reduced at the user's will.

ranges.summary: Up to 1 mile (1.6 km) for anyone able to hear; reducible to 150 feet (45.7 m).

duration.summary: Instant cry; demoralization lasts 1D4 melee rounds.

save.summary: Save vs Horror Factor equal to user's HF plus P.P.E. boost (maximum save required 18). HF above 18 counteracts victim save bonuses 1:1.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: 1D4×10% of enemies flee. Remainder fight at -1 melee attack; all combat bonuses halved for 1D4 melee rounds.

attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Uses half of the Nightbane's attacks for that melee round.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Up to 1 mile (1.6 km) away (must be able to hear it). Can be reduced to 150 feet (45.7 m) at the user's will.
rangeAndArea.notes: Minimum area 150 feet (45.7 m) when reduced.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Save vs user's Horror Factor + P.P.E. boost (5 P.P.E. per +1 HF). Maximum save required is 18; HF above 18 counteracts victim save bonuses on a 1:1 basis.

statusAlterations.summary: On a failed save: 1D4x10% of enemies run away. The rest fight at -1 melee attack and all combat bonuses are reduced by half for 1D4 melee rounds.

prerequisites.summary: Level 4 or greater and at least one feature from the Ancient Warrior, Dinosaur, or Fantasy table.

rangeMechanics.summary: Affects non-allies up to 1 mile (1.6 km) who can hear the cry; reducible to 150 feet (45.7 m).
rangeMechanics.notes: User may voluntarily reduce area to as little as 150 feet (45.7 m).

activationTime.summary: 5 P.P.E. per +1 Horror Factor boost (volume and ferocity scale with spend).

ppe.notes: 5 P.P.E. per +1 Horror Factor.

powerModes.effectsDescription: Raises user's Horror Factor for the save. Max save imposed 18; HF above 18 negates victim save bonuses 1:1. Failed save: 1D4×10% flee; remainder -1 attack and half combat bonuses for 1D4 melees.

penaltiesOnCaster.attacksPerMelee: Uses half of the Nightbane's attacks for that melee round.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.attacksPerMelee: -1 for those who fail but do not flee
penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: All combat bonuses halved for 1D4 melee rounds on failed save. 1D4×10% of affected enemies flee entirely.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: 1D4×10% flee; remainder -1 melee attack and all combat bonuses halved for 1D4 melee rounds.
savingThrow.consequenceOfSuccess: No effect.
savingThrow.notes: Not effective vs Nightlords, Avatars, Night Princes/Princesses, Greater Demons, or allies/supporters. 5 P.P.E. per +1 HF; HF above 18 counters save bonuses 1:1 while save remains 18+.

name: Zombie Master
description: Animates and controls the dead — less powerful than Necromantic magic. Zombies use Create Zombie ritual statistics (Through the Glass Darkly, p. 59). Up to one human or animal corpse per level (11 P.P.E. each; all may be animated in the same action). Mortal dead only, except Nightbane who died in human Facade form. Minions must stay within 300 feet (91.4 m). If the Nightbane is incapacitated or killed, zombies continue their last order until destroyed or duration ends.

enhancement.summary: 11 P.P.E. per additional corpse animated (up to one per level of experience; all may be animated in the same action).

ppe.notes: Eleven P.P.E. animates one dead creature; additional corpses cost 11 P.P.E. each up to one per level.

cannotAffect: Supernatural beings (except Nightbane who died in Facade form)
limitations.otherLimitations: One corpse (human or animal) per level of experience. Mortal dead only, except Nightbane who died in human Facade. Animated dead must stay within 300 feet (91.4 m).

ranges.summary: Animated dead must stay within 300 feet (91.4 m) of the Nightbane

duration.summary: Five minutes per level of experience.

prerequisites.summary: Only Nightbane with an Infernal or Undead Morphus may acquire this Talent.

rangeMechanics.summary: Animated dead must stay within 300 feet (91.4 m) of the Nightbane.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Zombie leash — 300 feet (91.4 m) from the Nightbane.

activationTime.summary: 11 P.P.E. per corpse animated; additional corpses (up to one per level) may all be raised in the same action.

createdObjects.name: Animated Dead
createdObjects.notes: Statistics equivalent to the Create Zombie ritual (Through the Glass Darkly, p. 59). Human or animal corpses only.

swarmMechanics.notes: One corpse per level of experience. All may be animated in a single action. Minions continue their last order if the Nightbane is incapacitated or killed until destroyed or duration elapses.

passengerCompanionCapacity.summary: Up to one animated corpse per level of experience.
passengerCompanionCapacity.notes: 11 P.P.E. per zombie; multiple corpses may be raised in the same activation.

combatMechanics.notes: Controlled zombies act on the Nightbane's orders. Last issued order persists if the creator is incapacitated or slain until zombies are destroyed or the Talent duration ends.

statusAlterations.summary: Dead mortals (or Facade-dead Nightbane) rise as obedient zombies under the Nightbane's control.
